text.title: onboarding-60-90-day Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for onboarding-60-90-day as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Defining "contributing" as meeting attendance or activity counts rather than outcome delivery.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Activity-based milestones (attended 10 standups, joined 3 cross-team meetings) create false completion signals. A hire who attends all meetings but delivers nothing has not demonstrated contribution — they have demonstrated availability.

failure-mode.description: Assigning a project so small it provides no meaningful evidence of delivery capability.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 2-hour task completed in week 5 does not demonstrate the planning, execution, and communication behaviors the phase is designed to reveal. Scope the first project to require 1-2 weeks of sustained effort.

failure-mode.description: Using the same milestone pace for all hires regardless of role level or prior experience.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A senior hire from an adjacent company may complete the Contribute phase in 3 weeks rather than 4-8. Rigidly enforcing the standard cadence frustrates fast ramps and signals the organization cannot adapt to individual capability.

failure-mode.description: Skipping the day-45 skip-level because the manager reports the hire is doing well.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The skip-level's purpose is precisely to check when the manager reports success. Confident reports from the manager are the highest-risk scenario for a blind-spot review at day 90.
